mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents through standardized communication protocols. It simplifies deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead for agent-based applications. Ideal for organizations looking to streamline their AI agent deployments in production environments.

steps.title: Add the Kagenti Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Kagenti Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Kagenti easily.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Kagenti repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Kagenti CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Kagenti to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/crds/kagenti.io_kagent_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Kagenti using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti application in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --create-namespace --version 0.5.1

```

> This command installs Kagenti in the 'kagenti' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Kagenti Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) to demonstrate how to deploy an AI agent using Kagenti.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: kagenti/agent:latest
  replicas: 1
EOF

```

> This command creates an example agent resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: kagenti/agent:latest
  replicas: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the deployed Custom Resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get kagent example-agent -o yaml

```

> This command retrieves the details of the example agent.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti installation and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> Uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> Deletes the namespace and any remaining resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step will upgrade Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get kagent -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --version 0.5.1

kubectl get kagent example-agent -o yaml

```

> Backup existing Custom Resources.
> Upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verify the status of the upgraded Custom Resource.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KAgent not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified KAgent resource does not exist in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure the KAgent resource is created correctly and check the namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image for the KAgent is not available or incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in the KAgent specification.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm release for Kagenti has not been installed or was deleted.

**Fix:**
Reinstall Kagenti using the Helm install command.

troubleshooting.title: KAgent is not running
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KAgent pod may have failed to start due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs for errors and ensure sufficient resources are allocated.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agent types and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KAgent resource in a 'Running' state when executing 'kubectl get kagent'.

  replicas: 1
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
